title: pteridium aquilinum subsp. wightianum
intro: Pteridium aquilinum subsp. wightianum, also known as the Himalayan Brake Fern, is a subspecies of the Bracken fern, a type of evergreen fern native to the Himalayas and other parts of Asia. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Pteridium aquilinum subsp. wightianum include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and support immune function. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and powders, which can be consumed internally to promote overall well-being.
